records.ety: [NL., fr. Gr. <?/ with two wings, di- = di`s- twice + <?/ feather, wing: cf. F. diptre.]

defs: An extensive order of insects having only two functional wings and two balancers, as the house fly, mosquito, etc. They have a suctorial proboscis, often including two pairs of sharp organs (mandibles and maxillæ) with which they pierce the skin of animals. They undergo a complete metamorphosis, their larvæ (called maggots) being usually without feet.
